notable_pick_trades: Traded original 5th-round pick to CLE for QB Kenny Pickett. Acquired 4th-round pick from MIN, 6th-round pick from NYJ. Projected 4th-round and 5th-round compensatory picks. Received 4th and 6th-round picks from JAX in Jakobi Meyers trade.
roster_context: The Raiders went 3-14 in 2025, earning the No. 1 overall pick. New HC Klint Kubiak (replacing fired Pete Carroll) and DC Rob Leonard are installing a 3-4 defense. GM John Spytek is entering year two with ~$88M in cap space and 10 draft picks. The franchise is in full rebuild mode around young core pieces Ashton Jeanty (RB), Brock Bowers (TE), and the expected No. 1 pick QB Fernando Mendoza. Geno Smith is expected to be released or traded before March 11 to avoid $8M additional guarantee. Maxx Crosby trade rumors are swirling — FOX Sports Jay Glazer reported Crosby wants out, and a trade could happen this week. Raiders are asking for two first-round picks and a player. Christian Wilkins was released in July 2025. The OL was decimated by injuries (Kolton Miller and Jackson Powers-Johnson both went to IR). The 2025 offense was historically bad.

needs.explanation: Geno Smith threw a league-high 17 interceptions in 2025 and is expected to be released/traded before the league year opens March 11 to avoid an additional $8M guarantee trigger. Kenny Pickett (FA) is not the answer either. The Raiders hold the No. 1 overall pick and Indiana QB Fernando Mendoza is the consensus selection — every major mock has him going to Las Vegas. This is the franchise's most critical need.
needs.current_starter: Geno Smith (likely to be released/traded), Kenny Pickett (UFA)
needs.depth: No viable QB on roster after Smith/Pickett departures

needs.explanation: After trading Jakobi Meyers to JAX mid-season, the Raiders have no true WR1. Tre Tucker had 57 catches for 696 yards but proved he's best as a WR2/3 — he disappeared when given the top role. Jack Bech and Dont'e Thornton Jr. are unproven second-year players. Tyler Lockett is a UFA. This group desperately needs an alpha WR to pair with Mendoza. A trade for a veteran (DJ Moore, AJ Brown, etc. have been floated alongside a potential Crosby deal) and/or a high draft pick are both likely.
needs.current_starter: Tre Tucker, Jack Bech, Dont'e Thornton Jr.
needs.depth: Shedrick Jackson, Alex Bachman — practice squad-caliber depth
needs.draft_range_target: Round 2 or free agency/trade. This is a historically deep WR draft class.

needs.explanation: The secondary was a major weakness in 2025. CB1 Eric Stokes is a UFA and could leave for a contender. Even if re-signed, the other two starting CB spots are question marks. Darien Porter (2025 3rd-rounder) started 10 games but is still developing. Darnay Holmes is a below-average starter. Kyu Blu Kelly is an ERFA with flashes but unproven. The Raiders were exploited through the air all season. Switching to a 3-4 adds complexity. The Raiders met with multiple DBs at the Combine.
needs.current_starter: Eric Stokes (UFA), Darien Porter, Darnay Holmes
needs.depth: Kyu Blu Kelly (ERFA), Sam Webb — very thin
needs.draft_range_target: Round 2-3, plus at least one FA signing

needs.explanation: If Maxx Crosby is traded — which multiple insiders say is increasingly likely — this becomes a Tier 1 need overnight. Crosby had 73 tackles, 28 TFL and 10 sacks in 2025 despite a knee injury. Malcolm Koonce is a UFA coming off an inconsistent season. Tyree Wilson has been a disappointment as a former top-10 pick, managing only 4.0 sacks in 2025. Even if Crosby stays, the Raiders need a credible second edge rusher. The switch to 3-4 means Wilson may move inside to 4i-technique. This is a very deep EDGE draft and FA class.
needs.current_starter: Maxx Crosby (may be traded), Tyree Wilson
needs.depth: Malcolm Koonce (UFA), Charles Snowden, Tonka Hemingway — thin if Crosby leaves
needs.draft_range_target: Round 2-3 (or Round 1 if Crosby traded and Raiders acquire additional first)

needs.explanation: LT Kolton Miller is under contract through 2028 but missed most of 2025 with a high ankle sprain/hairline fracture (IR from Week 4). He turns 31 in 2026 and durability is a concern. RT DJ Glaze may not fit Kubiak's wide zone scheme. Stone Forsythe (UFA) was a poor replacement. Third-round picks Charles Grant and Caleb Rogers were barely active as rookies. The Raiders need a RT who fits the zone scheme and OT depth insurance.
needs.depth: Charles Grant, Caleb Rogers — both unproven 2025 3rd-rounders
needs.draft_range_target: Round 2-4. Strong OT draft class with five projected first-rounders.

needs.explanation: Christian Wilkins was released in July 2025 after a messy contract dispute. The interior DL was a weakness all season. Adam Butler is aging (32) and production dipped; he may be cut. Jonah Laulu showed flashes but is not a difference-maker. Tonka Hemingway (2025 4th-rounder) had 4 sacks in limited action and fits the new 3-4 scheme. The switch to a 3-4 requires a quality nose tackle, which the Raiders lack. Thomas Booker (RFA) is a question mark.
needs.depth: Tonka Hemingway, Leki Fotu, Thomas Booker (RFA) — adequate depth but no anchor
needs.draft_range_target: Round 3-5 or free agency for a nose tackle

needs.explanation: The linebacker group was a glaring weakness in 2025. The Raiders were 29th in DVOA on passes to the short middle of the field. Devin White (UFA) made a high volume of tackles but was a liability in coverage. Elandon Roberts (UFA) is a run-stuffing thumper with no range. Germaine Pratt is the only returning starter. The 3-4 scheme demands athletic off-ball LBs who can cover. This is a critical scheme fit need.
needs.current_starter: Germaine Pratt, Devin White (UFA), Elandon Roberts (UFA)
needs.depth: Tommy Eichenberg, Amari Gainer, Jamin Davis — unproven

needs.explanation: C/G Jackson Powers-Johnson was placed on IR with an ankle injury in Week 10 and missed the rest of 2025. He's under contract and expected to be the starting center, but health is a concern entering year three. LG Dylan Parham is a UFA. RG Jordan Meredith is a RFA. The Raiders invested 2025 3rd-round pick Caleb Rogers at guard but he was barely active. Alex Cappa was poor in 2025. If Parham and Meredith leave, there's a real gap at guard, but the draft picks from 2025 could step up.
needs.current_starter: Jackson Powers-Johnson (C), Dylan Parham (UFA, LG), Jordan Meredith (RFA, C/G)
needs.depth: Caleb Rogers, Will Putnam (ERFA), Alex Cappa — below average

needs.explanation: Jeremy Chinn is under contract and was solid in 2025. Isaiah Pola-Mao started alongside him but is a limited starter. Jamal Adams (UFA) and Lonnie Johnson Jr. (UFA) are not returning pieces. Tristin McCollum (ERFA) provides depth. Safety is not the most pressing need but lacks a true playmaking FS to pair with Chinn. The Raiders met with DBs at the Combine including South Carolina's Brandon Cisse.
needs.depth: Tristin McCollum (ERFA), Terrell Edmunds — adequate but not impact players

free_agency_impact: Geno Smith expected to be released/traded before March 11 to avoid $8M guarantee trigger — makes QB the clear No. 1 overall pick
Maxx Crosby trade increasingly likely (FOX's Glazer says he wants out; Raiders asking 2 firsts + a player) — would massively elevate EDGE need and could bring back WR + draft capital
CB Eric Stokes (UFA) — top CB1, Raiders want to re-sign but he has outside suitors. Loss would make CB even more critical
EDGE Malcolm Koonce (UFA) — decent rotational rusher, likely to depart, leaving EDGE thin
LB Devin White (UFA) and LB Elandon Roberts (UFA) — both expected to depart, creating LB void
OG Dylan Parham (UFA) and C Jordan Meredith (RFA) — OL continuity at risk if both leave
K Daniel Carlson (UFA) — solid kicker, would like to retain
QB Kenny Pickett (UFA) — not re-signing, was acquired as a backup from CLE
WR Tyler Lockett (UFA) — veteran signed mid-season, not expected back
Christian Wilkins released in July 2025 in contentious contract dispute — created a hole at IDL that was never filled
Jakobi Meyers traded to JAX at 2025 deadline for 4th and 6th-round picks — created WR void
RB Raheem Mostert (UFA) — minimal role in 2025, likely not returning
Signed RB Raheem Mostert (1-yr) and acquired Geno Smith trade + extension pre-2025, but both moves failed — lessons learned for rebuild approach
scheme_fit_notes: New HC Klint Kubiak runs a wide zone offense (Shanahan/Kubiak tree) that prioritizes OL athleticism and movement skills — LT Kolton Miller and C Jackson Powers-Johnson fit, but RT DJ Glaze may not. New DC Rob Leonard is installing a 3-4 defense, which shifts Crosby to standup OLB, Wilson to 4i-technique inside, and demands athletic off-ball LBs who can cover. The 3-4 also requires a true nose tackle the Raiders currently lack. CB prospects with length and physicality are valued. Zone-blocking OL and coverage-capable LBs should be prioritized in the draft.
